status_notes: Initial research from Azure Blob Storage documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs) and partial Pass 2 (API). Needs Pass 3 (cross-reference) and Pass 5 (human review with live Azure environment).
notes: Object storage for Azure. Part of Azure Storage account. Security includes encryption, RBAC, SAS tokens, soft delete, immutability, private endpoints.

match_nodes.description: Azure Storage encryption at rest for blob data

data.description: Azure Storage automatically encrypts all data at rest using 256-bit AES encryption. Three key management options available, plus an infrastructure encryption option for double encryption.

options.name: Microsoft-managed keys
options.description: Default. Microsoft manages encryption keys. No configuration required.

options.name: Customer-managed keys (CMK)
options.description: Keys stored in Azure Key Vault or Managed HSM. Customer controls key rotation and access.

options.name: Infrastructure encryption (double encryption)
options.description: Second layer of encryption at the infrastructure level using platform-managed keys. Combined with service-level encryption for two layers.

default.note: All data encrypted by default with Microsoft-managed keys. No opt-out possible.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use customer-managed keys for full key lifecycle control. Enable infrastructure encryption for compliance scenarios requiring double encryption.

match_nodes.description: Public/anonymous read access to blob containers and data

data.description: Controls whether blobs and containers can be accessed without authentication. Can be disabled at the storage account level to prevent any container from being configured for public access.

options.description: No anonymous access allowed. Recommended setting.
Anonymous read access for container and blob data.
Anonymous read access for blob data only (must know exact blob name).

default.note: Anonymous access is disabled by default for new storage accounts.

data.vendor_recommendation: Disable anonymous access at the storage account level. Use Entra ID (RBAC) or SAS tokens for authorized access.

audit.console: Storage account → Settings → Configuration → Allow Blob anonymous access

match_nodes.description: Azure RBAC for data plane access to blob storage

data.description: Use Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) and Azure RBAC for data plane access to blobs. Provides identity-based, auditable access control. Can be set as the default authentication method for the storage account.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use Entra ID (RBAC) instead of shared keys or SAS tokens. Set defaultToOAuthAuthentication to true.

audit.console: Storage account → Settings → Configuration → Default to Microsoft Entra authorization

configure.note: Assign built-in roles: Storage Blob Data Reader, Storage Blob Data Contributor, Storage Blob Data Owner

data.description: Two 512-bit access keys per storage account that provide full access to all data. Can be disabled to force Entra ID (RBAC) authentication only.

options.description: Default. Shared key authorization allowed.
Shared key authorization blocked. Forces Entra ID (OAuth) authentication.

default.note: Shared key authorization is enabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Disallow shared key authorization to enforce Entra ID authentication. Rotate access keys regularly if shared key must remain enabled.

audit.console: Storage account → Settings → Configuration → Allow storage account key access

match_nodes.description: Delegated access tokens (SAS) for time-limited blob access

data.description: Shared Access Signatures provide delegated, time-limited access to storage resources. Three types with different security properties.

options.description: Backed by Entra ID credentials. Most secure. Recommended. Only works with Blob and Data Lake storage.
Backed by storage account key. Scoped to a single service (Blob, Queue, Table, File).
Backed by storage account key. Can delegate access across multiple services.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use user delegation SAS whenever possible. It is backed by Entra ID credentials and does not require distributing storage account keys.

match_nodes.description: Recoverable deletion for blobs and containers

data.description: Maintains deleted data in a recoverable state for a specified retention period. Available for both blobs and containers independently.

options.description: Retains deleted blobs and blob versions. Configurable retention 1-365 days.
Retains deleted containers. Configurable retention 1-365 days.

default.note: Blob soft delete and container soft delete are enabled by default with 7-day retention for new storage accounts.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable both blob and container soft delete with a retention period that meets your recovery requirements.

audit.cli: az storage account blob-service-properties show --account-name {acct} --query deleteRetentionPolicy
audit.cli_container: az storage account blob-service-properties show --account-name {acct} --query containerDeleteRetentionPolicy

configure.cli: az storage account blob-service-properties update --account-name {acct} --resource-group {rg} --enable-delete-retention true --delete-retention-days 30

match_nodes.description: WORM (Write Once Read Many) immutability for blob data

data.description: Prevents modification and deletion of blob data. Two policy types: time-based retention (locked for a specified period) and legal hold (locked until explicitly removed). Can be applied at container or version level.

options.description: Objects cannot be modified or deleted for the specified retention period. Policy can be locked (irreversible).
Objects cannot be modified or deleted until all legal holds are cleared. No retention period — holds are explicit.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use immutability policies for regulatory compliance (SEC 17a-4, CFTC 1.31). Lock time-based retention policies to make them irreversible.

audit.console: Storage account → Containers → {container} → Access policy → Immutable blob storage

configure.terraform: azurerm_storage_container (has no direct immutability resource; use azapi_resource)

match_nodes.description: Blob versioning to maintain previous object versions

data.description: Automatically maintains previous versions of a blob when it is modified or deleted. Each version has a unique version ID. Works with soft delete for comprehensive data protection.

default.note: Blob versioning is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable versioning for data protection. Combine with soft delete and lifecycle management to control version retention costs.

audit.cli: az storage account blob-service-properties show --account-name {acct} --query isVersioningEnabled

configure.cli: az storage account blob-service-properties update --account-name {acct} --resource-group {rg} --enable-versioning true

match_nodes.description: Require HTTPS for storage account connections

data.description: Enforces secure transfer (HTTPS) for all requests to the storage account. Rejects any requests made over HTTP.

default.note: Secure transfer is enabled by default for new storage accounts.

data.vendor_recommendation: Keep secure transfer enabled. Never disable it.

audit.console: Storage account → Settings → Configuration → Secure transfer required

match_nodes.description: Minimum TLS version for storage account connections

data.description: Sets the minimum version of TLS that clients must use when connecting to the storage account. Requests using older TLS versions are rejected.

options.description: TLS 1.0 minimum. Not recommended.
TLS 1.1 minimum. Not recommended.
TLS 1.2 minimum. Recommended.

default.note: TLS 1.2 is the default minimum for new storage accounts.

data.vendor_recommendation: Set minimum TLS version to 1.2. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 have known vulnerabilities.

audit.console: Storage account → Settings → Configuration → Minimum TLS version

match_nodes.description: Private connectivity to blob storage via Azure Private Link

data.description: Provides private connectivity from a virtual network to the storage account over the Microsoft backbone network. Traffic never traverses the public internet. Uses a private IP address from the VNet address space.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use private endpoints for storage accounts that contain sensitive data. Combine with network rules to deny public access.

audit.console: Storage account → Security + networking → Networking → Private endpoint connections

match_nodes.description: Microsoft Defender threat detection for storage accounts

data.description: Security intelligence layer that detects unusual and potentially harmful attempts to access or exploit storage accounts. Detects anomalous access patterns, anonymous access from suspicious IPs, unusual data extraction, and suspicious uploads (malware scanning).

options.description: Detects anomalous access patterns, unauthorized access, suspicious data extraction
Scans uploaded blobs for malware using Microsoft Defender Antivirus engine
Prioritizes alerts for resources containing sensitive data

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Defender for Storage on all storage accounts, especially those containing sensitive data.

audit.console: Storage account → Security → Microsoft Defender for Cloud
audit.note: Can also be viewed at subscription level in Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

configure.cli: az security pricing create --name StorageAccounts --tier Standard
configure.note: Can be enabled at subscription level (all storage accounts) or per-account.
